description: Interviews from the period surrounding Drive, focused on style, restraint, violence, and screen presence.

items.title: Cannes Conversation
items.quote: I was drawn to the stillness of the character.
items.summary: A discussion of silence, gesture, and the role of visual cool in shaping the character.

items.title: Craft Profile
items.quote: Sometimes what you don't say becomes the performance.
items.summary: An interview focusing on minimalism, physical acting, and the power of withholding emotion.

description: Interviews focused on politics, ambition, performance control, and the tension between image and sincerity.

items.title: Press Interview
items.quote: He believes in the image until the image starts collapsing.
items.summary: A discussion of power, image-management, and the emotional instability beneath political confidence.

era: The Place Beyond the Pines Era
description: Interviews centred on masculinity, fatherhood, emotional damage, and the film's broader dramatic ambition.

items.title: Press Junket Interview
items.quote: The character is driven by love, but in a destructive way.
items.summary: A conversation about fractured masculinity, family, and why the role feels emotionally raw.

description: Interviews from the La La Land period, often focusing on music, romance, performance chemistry, and classic Hollywood influence.

items.title: Awards Season Feature
items.quote: It had to feel nostalgic, but also alive.
items.summary: An interview discussing music, rehearsal, romance, and the balance between stylisation and sincerity.

description: Interviews focused on atmosphere, interiority, science fiction, and the emotional compression of the role.

items.title: The Hollywood Reporter Interview
items.quote: I like characters who are a little bit damaged.
items.summary: A discussion of inwardness, ambiguity, and why emotionally fractured characters are compelling.

description: Interviews from the Barbie period, often playful in tone and centred on comedy, masculinity, performance image, and public visibility.

items.title: Vogue Feature
items.quote: Ken only exists if Barbie looks at him.
items.summary: A playful interview exploring comedy, self-awareness, and the strange emotional logic of Ken.

items.title: Press Conference
items.quote: You have to commit completely to the absurdity.
items.summary: A lively press conversation about humour, sincerity, spectacle, and why the role resonated so strongly.
